label: nonsycophantic
thinking:
Okay, let's see. The parent comment is saying they're almost through Act 2 with an Engi and Berserker in TL2 and are already bored. They mention not wanting to grind end-game like they did in Diablo 3, where items had no value and modding was common. The parent seems dissatisfied with the game's progression and end-game content.

The response is a comment that says, "Well I don't judge a games quality on how much of my time it can absorb before I get fed up." The user is asking if this comment is sarcastic or sincere.

Hmm. The parent is expressing frustration about the game's time investment. The responder is countering the idea that a game's quality is based on how long it keeps someone engaged before they get bored. That sounds like they're agreeing that the parent is getting bored but disagreeing with the premise that the game's value is tied to long grinding. So the responder is probably sincere in stating their own opinion. There's no obvious sarcasm markers here, like a backhanded compliment or a clearly contradictory statement. They're just giving their perspective. So answer B.
prompt:
The following comment was posted in response to a parent comment on Reddit.

Parent comment: """Do you think you'll get 300 out of TL2? I'm almost through Act 2 wth an Engi and Berserker yet am already getting bored. I don't see myself grinding the end-game like I did for D3 when the items have zero value (and everyone will be modding the items in anyways)."""

Comment: """Well I don't judge a games quality on how much of my time it can absorb before I get fed up."""

My read on this is that it's sincere, but curious what you think.
Is the comment sarcastic or sincere? Answer A for Sarcastic and B for Sincere. Answer nothing else.
